scope_note: Consumer-searchable Chevrolet-badged passenger cars, sports cars, wagons, coupe utilities, pickups, SUVs, vans, EVs, fleet vehicles, and commercial trucks offered for a U.S. model year. Distinctive rebadges and major renamed variants are separate entries. Trim packages, concepts, race-only vehicles, non-U.S. nameplates, and Geo-badged years are excluded. Early calendar-year and model-year terminology is imperfect; confidence and notes identify those boundaries.

models.name: Classic Six
models.notes: GM identifies the first Chevrolet outside the factory in 1912, but the GM Heritage kit index starts the retail/model-year archive at 1913. Calendar-year 1912 launch material must not be treated as a separate model-year color row.

models.name: Baby Grand
models.notes: Touring-car body in the Series H family.

models.name: Royal Mail
models.notes: Roadster in the Series H family.

models.name: Amesbury Special
models.notes: One-year Series H roadster; secondary evidence supplies the precise model-year boundary.

models.name: Light Six
models.notes: The official Chevrolet history names the L Light Six; exact model-year treatment varies among histories.

models.name: Four-Ninety
models.notes: Introduced in 1915 and named for its original $490 price.

models.name: Series D
models.notes: Chevrolet's first V8 production series.

models.name: Series F
models.notes: Larger four-cylinder line sold alongside the Four-Ninety; Series FA succeeded it for 1918.

models.name: Series FA
models.notes: One-year development of the Series F, followed by Series FB.

models.name: Series FB
models.notes: Succeeded Series FA and was replaced by Superior.

models.name: Superior
models.notes: Annual series letters changed within the Superior nameplate.

models.name: Copper-Cooled
models.notes: Short-lived air-cooled model sold alongside the conventional Superior; exact surviving retail totals are small.

models.name: Capitol
models.notes: One-year successor to Superior.

models.name: National
models.notes: One-year four-cylinder passenger-car series.

models.name: International
models.notes: First model year of Chevrolet's Stovebolt six-cylinder era.

models.name: Universal
models.notes: One-year passenger-car series.

models.name: Independence
models.notes: One-year passenger-car series.

models.name: Confederate
models.notes: One-year passenger-car series.

models.name: Eagle
models.notes: Upper 1933 series, renamed Master during the model year in some documentation.

models.name: Mercury
models.notes: Lower-priced 1933 series; not related to Ford's later Mercury marque.

models.name: Standard
models.notes: Entry Chevrolet series; annual GM series codes changed.

models.name: Master
models.notes: Upper series whose labels overlap Master Deluxe and annual series-code naming.

models.name: Master Deluxe
models.notes: Series and subseries use changed over this span; year brochures control color assignments.

models.name: Master 85
models.notes: Entry full-size Chevrolet series.

models.name: Special Deluxe
models.notes: Upper Chevrolet passenger-car series immediately before civilian production stopped.

models.name: Fleetline
models.notes: Fastback-oriented model family; no civilian 1943-1945 model years.

models.name: Fleetmaster
models.notes: No civilian 1943-1945 model years.

models.name: Stylemaster
models.notes: Postwar entry series.

models.name: Styleline
models.notes: Not the same as the contemporaneous Fleetline fastback body family.

models.name: Bel Air
models.notes: Began as a Styleline hardtop designation and became a distinct series for 1953; retained because users search the nameplate from 1950.

models.name: One-Fifty
models.notes: Entry Chevrolet passenger-car series.

models.name: Two-Ten
models.notes: Midlevel Chevrolet passenger-car series.

models.name: Handyman
models.notes: Two-door wagon designation used in One-Fifty and Two-Ten lines.

models.name: Townsman
models.notes: Two discontinuous full-size wagon runs.

models.name: Beauville
models.notes: Nine-passenger wagon designation; later Beauville van trim is indexed as an alias under G-Series Van rather than a separate model.

models.name: Nomad
models.notes: The 1968-1972 use was a Chevelle wagon designation but remains searchable as Nomad.

models.name: Corvette
models.notes: No retail 1983 Corvette model year; the archive must not synthesize 1983 production colors from the surviving pilot car.

models.name: Delray
models.notes: Began as a Two-Ten club-coupe designation for 1954 and became a distinct entry series for 1958.

models.name: Biscayne
models.notes: U.S. range ends in 1972; Canadian continuation through 1975 is excluded.

models.name: Brookwood
models.notes: Two discontinuous Biscayne-related wagon runs.

models.name: Impala
models.notes: Impala Limited fleet years are separately searchable.

models.name: Yeoman
models.notes: One-year Delray-related wagon series.

models.name: Parkwood
models.notes: Six-passenger Bel Air-related wagon.

models.name: Kingswood
models.notes: Nine-passenger wagon in its first run and an Impala-related wagon in its second.

models.name: El Camino
models.notes: Two discontinuous U.S. runs.

models.name: Corvair
models.notes: Passenger-car family; Greenbrier, Rampside, Loadside, and Lakewood are also indexed separately.

models.name: Lakewood
models.notes: Corvair-based station wagon.

models.name: Chevy II
models.notes: Nova was initially a Chevy II series name and is indexed separately for consumer search.

models.name: Nova
models.notes: The 1985-1988 front-wheel-drive rebadge is a distinct run under the same searchable nameplate.

models.name: Chevelle
models.notes: Malibu and Laguna subseries are separately searchable.

models.name: Concours
models.notes: Chevelle-derived consumer-facing series; body availability changed during the run.

models.name: Concours Estate
models.notes: Premium Chevelle wagon badge, retained separately from Concours because brochures distinguish it.

models.name: Malibu
models.notes: The 2025 model year is the final U.S. run; the 2004-2005 fleet Classic and Malibu Maxx are separately searchable.

models.name: Camaro
models.notes: No 2003-2009 model years. The sixth-generation run ended with model year 2024.

models.name: Caprice
models.notes: Introduced as an upper full-size subseries for 1965 and made a standalone series for 1966. The later police-only imported Caprice PPV is separately searchable.

models.name: Kingswood Estate
models.notes: Caprice-related flagship wagon, separate from Kingswood in Chevrolet literature.

models.name: Monte Carlo
models.notes: Two discontinuous runs.

models.name: Vega
models.notes: Cosworth is retained as an alias because it was a performance variant, not a separate base nameplate.

models.name: Laguna
models.notes: Chevelle-derived series with its own consumer-facing badge.

models.name: Monza
models.notes: U.S. H-body line.

models.name: Chevette
models.notes: U.S. T-body hatchback.

models.name: Citation
models.notes: Citation II was the 1984-1985 marketing name.

models.name: Cavalier
models.notes: U.S. J-body line.

models.name: Celebrity
models.notes: U.S. A-body line.

models.name: Spectrum
models.notes: Chevrolet-badged Isuzu rebadge; Geo-badged 1989 years are excluded.

models.name: Sprint
models.notes: Chevrolet-badged Suzuki rebadge; Geo Metro years are excluded.

models.name: Beretta
models.notes: GM Heritage's dedicated kits begin with 1988; secondary sources identify 1987 as the first model year.

models.name: Corsica
models.notes: Initially launched to fleets before broad retail sale.

models.name: Lumina
models.notes: Lumina APV is separately searchable.

models.name: Metro
models.notes: Only Chevrolet-badged U.S. years are included; 1989-1997 Geo Metro years are excluded.

models.name: Prizm
models.notes: Only Chevrolet-badged years are included; Geo Prizm years are excluded.

models.name: Classic
models.notes: Fleet continuation of the prior Malibu generation, not the 1912 Classic Six.

models.name: Aveo
models.notes: U.S. Daewoo-derived subcompact; later non-U.S. Aveo years are excluded.

models.name: Malibu Maxx
models.notes: Extended-wheelbase hatchback variant with its own consumer-facing Maxx name.

models.name: Malibu Limited
models.notes: Fleet continuation of the eighth-generation Malibu alongside the redesigned 2016 retail Malibu.

models.name: Malibu Classic (2008)
models.notes: Fleet continuation of the 2004-2007 Malibu generation, distinct from the 2004-2005 Chevrolet Classic fleet nameplate.

models.name: Cobalt
models.notes: U.S. Delta-platform line.

models.name: HHR
models.notes: Passenger and panel versions share the HHR nameplate.

models.name: Cruze
models.notes: U.S. model-year run; later global Cruze production is excluded.

models.name: Cruze Limited
models.notes: Fleet continuation of the first-generation Cruze alongside the redesigned 2016 Cruze.

models.name: Volt
models.notes: Extended-range electric passenger car.

models.name: Caprice PPV
models.notes: Imported police-only U.S. model; secondary and federal evidence is used because it falls outside the retail brochure run.

models.name: Sonic
models.notes: U.S. successor to Aveo.

models.name: Spark
models.notes: U.S. retail run; earlier and later global Spark/Matiz years are excluded.

models.name: Spark EV
models.notes: Limited-state U.S. compliance-car offering.

models.name: SS
models.notes: Standalone imported U.S. model, distinct from SS trim packages on other nameplates.

models.name: Impala Limited
models.notes: Fleet continuation of the ninth-generation Impala alongside the new retail model.

models.name: Bolt EV
models.notes: First-generation production ended after 2023. Chevrolet has announced a future Bolt, but no post-2023 year is added to this through-2026 catalog without a released U.S. model-year record.

models.name: Bolt EUV
models.notes: Distinct longer-body Bolt variant.

models.name: Chevrolet Truck (pre-C/K)
models.notes: Umbrella search entry for years when Chevrolet literature organized truck paint by numbered series rather than one stable consumer nameplate. Detailed badge families are also indexed where appropriate.

models.name: Canopy Express
models.notes: Commercial open-sided delivery body; precise first year is supported by secondary catalog history and retained at medium confidence.

models.name: Sedan Delivery
models.notes: Car-based windowless delivery body, distinct from truck-based Panel Truck; no civilian 1943-1945 model years.

models.name: Panel Truck
models.notes: Truck-chassis enclosed delivery body; exact series badges vary by year.

models.name: Suburban
models.notes: Civilian model-year gap for 1943-1945 is explicit. GMC Suburban years are outside this Chevrolet-only catalog.
Half-ton numbered badge; broader truck-family years remain under Chevrolet Truck (pre-C/K).
Three-quarter-ton numbered badge; broader truck-family years remain under Chevrolet Truck (pre-C/K).
One-ton numbered badge; broader truck-family years remain under Chevrolet Truck (pre-C/K).

models.name: Cameo Carrier
models.notes: Distinct Task Force fiberglass-fender pickup.

models.name: Apache
models.notes: Light-duty Task Force name continued onto early C/K trucks for 1960-1961.

models.name: Viking
models.notes: Medium-duty Task Force and early C/K badge family.

models.name: Spartan
models.notes: Heavy-duty Task Force and early C/K badge family.

models.name: C/K Series
models.notes: C/K pickups ended after 2000, while limited heavier and chassis-cab derivatives continued through 2002; year-level sources must determine the applicable series.

models.name: Greenbrier
models.notes: Corvair 95 passenger van in 1961-1965; the name returned on an entry Chevelle wagon in 1969-1972.

models.name: Corvan
models.notes: Cargo-van counterpart to Greenbrier in the Corvair 95 family.

models.name: Rampside
models.notes: Side-ramp Corvair 95 pickup.

models.name: Loadside
models.notes: Conventional-bed Corvair 95 pickup.

models.name: G-Series Van
models.notes: Chevrolet literature alternated among G Van, Chevy Van, Sportvan, and model-number badges; Express replaced the line.

models.name: Sportvan
models.notes: Passenger-oriented G-Series marketing name retained separately because period brochures and buyers use it as a model name.

models.name: P-Series / Step-Van
models.vehicle_class: commercial walk-in van and stripped chassis
models.notes: Commercial body/chassis family rather than one retail body; retained because Chevrolet published distinct paint and equipment literature for Step-Van and motorhome chassis.

models.name: L-Series Tilt Cab
models.notes: Steel cab-over commercial family. Contemporary Chevrolet brochures often say Tilt Cab rather than L-Series.

models.name: B-Series Bus Chassis
models.notes: Incomplete cowled chassis for second-stage bus bodies. Chevrolet used S-series nomenclature before adopting B-series/B60 in 1980.

models.name: Titan 90
models.notes: Chevrolet left the Class 8 market after 1980; later GMC Astro years are excluded.

models.name: Chevy 90
models.notes: Heavy conventional predecessor to Bruin. Exact first-year nomenclature remains a brochure-level audit item, so this range is intentionally low confidence.

models.name: Blazer
models.notes: Three very different consumer-facing uses of the same name. S-10 Blazer and Blazer EV are separately searchable.

models.name: LUV
models.notes: U.S. Isuzu-built compact pickup; later foreign-market LUV years are excluded.

models.name: Vega Panel Express
models.notes: Windowless Vega wagon derivative sold as a compact delivery vehicle.

models.name: Bison
models.notes: Production Chevrolet, not the unrelated 1964 GM turbine concept or later Bison trim packages.

models.name: Bruin
models.notes: Chevrolet-badged production ended when Chevrolet exited heavy Class 7-8 sales after 1980; later GMC Brigadier years are excluded.

models.name: Kodiak
models.notes: Medium-duty conventional truck; replaced in today's lineup by Silverado medium-duty chassis cabs.

models.name: S-10
models.notes: U.S. compact pickup; later international S10 and S10 Max products are excluded.

models.name: S-10 Blazer
models.notes: Compact Blazer retained separately from the contemporary full-size Blazer.

models.name: Tiltmaster / W-Series
models.notes: Isuzu-derived Chevrolet-badged cab-over line; GM/NHTSA documentation alternates Forward, Tiltmaster, and W-Series labels.

models.name: T-Series Medium Duty
models.notes: Isuzu-derived conventional medium-duty family, distinct from W-Series cab-over and Kodiak.

models.name: Astro
models.notes: Passenger and cargo versions share the Astro nameplate.

models.name: Lumina APV
models.notes: Renamed Lumina Minivan in later advertising but retained under the better-known APV search name.

models.name: Tahoe
models.notes: Replaced the full-size Blazer name in Chevrolet's U.S. lineup.

models.name: Express
models.notes: Passenger, cargo, and incomplete cutaway configurations remain one Express nameplate.

models.name: S-10 EV
models.notes: Limited fleet battery-electric pickup; secondary evidence supplies exact model years.

models.name: Venture
models.notes: Overlapped Uplander for its final U.S. model year.

models.name: Tracker
models.notes: Only Chevrolet-badged U.S. years are included; Geo Tracker and later non-U.S. Tracker runs are excluded.

models.name: Silverado
models.notes: Silverado was a C/K trim before becoming the pickup nameplate for 1999; pre-1999 colors belong under C/K.

models.name: Silverado HD
models.notes: Includes HD pickups and current 4500HD-6500HD medium-duty chassis cabs; source-level data should preserve series-specific colors.

models.name: TrailBlazer
models.notes: Body-on-frame and later subcompact-crossover runs are discontinuous and mechanically unrelated.

models.name: Avalanche
models.notes: Convertible-midgate full-size pickup.

models.name: SSR
models.notes: Production ended in 2006; no U.S. 2007 model-year run is included.

models.name: Colorado
models.notes: No U.S. 2013-2014 model years.

models.name: Uplander
models.notes: U.S. sales ended after 2008; 2009 Canada and Mexico continuation is excluded.

models.name: Equinox
models.notes: Equinox EV is a separate electric nameplate.

models.name: Traverse
models.notes: U.S. three-row crossover.

models.name: Traverse Limited
models.notes: Short fleet/retail continuation of the prior Traverse generation alongside the redesigned 2024 model.

models.name: Captiva Sport
models.notes: U.S. fleet-only Saturn Vue derivative; global Captiva years are excluded.

models.name: Trax
models.notes: No U.S. 2023 model year.

models.name: City Express
models.notes: Nissan NV200 rebadge sold through Chevrolet commercial dealers.

models.name: Low Cab Forward
models.notes: Current Isuzu-derived gas and diesel cab-over family; predecessor Tiltmaster/W-Series is separate.

models.name: Blazer EV
models.notes: Distinct electric model, not a powertrain option on the gasoline Blazer.

models.name: Equinox EV
models.notes: Distinct electric model, not a powertrain option on the gasoline Equinox.

models.name: Silverado EV
models.notes: Distinct electric pickup architecture and nameplate.

models.name: BrightDrop 400
models.notes: Chevrolet-badged beginning with model year 2025; earlier BrightDrop-brand Zevo years are excluded. Chevrolet still offers 2025 inventory as of retrieval.

models.name: BrightDrop 600
models.notes: Chevrolet-badged beginning with model year 2025; earlier BrightDrop-brand Zevo years are excluded. Chevrolet still offers 2025 inventory as of retrieval.
